title: ericameria discoidea
intro: Ericameria discoidea, also known as rabbitbrush or goldenbrush, is a perennial shrub native to the western United States and Canada, characterized by its yellow flowers and silvery-gray foliage. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which are attributed to its essential oils and polyphenolic compounds. The health benefits of Ericameria discoidea include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve digestion, and support immune function, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy. Herbal preparations of the plant include teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be used to make topical applications for skin issues and internal remedies for various health concerns.
